label: DynamoDB
description: Query and write to Amazon DynamoDB tables using AWS credentials scoped to your account and region.

form.title: Connect DynamoDB
form.subtitle: Provide AWS credentials so Anton can read and write DynamoDB tables on your behalf.

methods.label: AWS Access Key
methods.description: Paste an IAM Access Key ID and Secret Access Key with DynamoDB permissions. The simplest and most common path — no SDK or CLI setup required.
methods.how_to:
## AWS Access Key (IAM credentials)

Create an IAM user (or use an existing one) scoped to DynamoDB, generate an access key, and paste the credentials below.

### Steps

1. Open the [AWS IAM Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/) and go to **Users**.
2. Click **Create user** (or pick an existing service user). Give it a name like `anton-dynamodb`.
3. On the **Permissions** step, attach the policy **AmazonDynamoDBFullAccess** — or create an inline policy with only the actions you need (`dynamodb:GetItem`, `dynamodb:PutItem`, `dynamodb:Query`, `dynamodb:Scan`, etc.).
4. Finish creating the user, then open the user → **Security credentials** tab → **Create access key**.
5. Choose **Application running outside AWS** as the use case.
6. Copy the **Access key ID** and **Secret access key** — the secret is shown only once.
7. Paste both values into the fields below, and set the **Region** to the AWS region where your DynamoDB tables live (e.g. `us-east-1`).

### Notes

- Prefer a dedicated IAM user with least-privilege permissions rather than using your root account credentials.
- If you lose the secret key, deactivate the old key in IAM and generate a new one — there is no way to retrieve it again.
- The region must match where your tables are provisioned; DynamoDB is a regional service.
- See [AWS docs on managing access keys](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html) for full details.

fields.name: access_key_id
fields.label: Access Key ID
fields.description: The AWS Access Key ID from IAM → Users → Security credentials.

fields.name: secret_access_key
fields.label: Secret Access Key
fields.description: The secret shown once when you created the access key. Keep this safe.

fields.name: region
fields.label: AWS Region
fields.description: The AWS region where your DynamoDB tables are provisioned.

fields.name: endpoint_url
fields.label: Custom Endpoint URL (optional)
fields.description: Override the default DynamoDB endpoint — useful for DynamoDB Local or LocalStack during development. Leave blank for AWS.

methods.label: IAM Role via Assumed Role Keys
methods.description: Use temporary STS credentials from an assumed IAM Role — Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, and a Session Token. Suitable for environments using role-based access or short-lived credentials.
methods.how_to:
## Assumed IAM Role (temporary STS credentials)

If your organization uses IAM Roles rather than long-lived IAM user keys, you can generate temporary credentials via AWS STS and paste them here.

### Steps

1. Identify the IAM Role ARN you want to assume (e.g. `arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/DynamoDBAccessRole`).
2. Run the following with the AWS CLI or SDK to obtain temporary credentials:
   - `aws sts assume-role --role-arn <ARN> --role-session-name anton-session`
3. The response includes `AccessKeyId`, `SecretAccessKey`, and `SessionToken`. Copy all three.
4. Paste each into the corresponding fields below.
5. Set the **Region** to match your DynamoDB tables.

### Notes

- Temporary STS credentials expire (default 1 hour, max 12 hours). You will need to repeat this process and update Anton when they expire.
- For a longer-lived setup without credential rotation headaches, use the **AWS Access Key** method with a dedicated IAM user instead.
- See [AWS STS AssumeRole docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/STS/latest/APIReference/API_AssumeRole.html) for full details.

fields.name: access_key_id
fields.label: Access Key ID
fields.description: The temporary Access Key ID returned by STS AssumeRole. Starts with ASIA.

fields.name: secret_access_key
fields.label: Secret Access Key
fields.description: The temporary Secret Access Key paired with the Access Key ID above.

fields.name: session_token
fields.label: Session Token
fields.description: The STS session token required alongside temporary credentials. This is a long string returned by AssumeRole.

fields.name: region
fields.label: AWS Region
fields.description: The AWS region where your DynamoDB tables are provisioned.
